choices.text: Frame it as the victim bringing trouble
Frame it as pressure and circumstance
Frame it as someone trying to protect something
Frame it as something that got out of hand

nodes.text: I know the difference between a stranger's movement and a familiar one. What I saw near {place} was familiar enough to hurt before it made sense.
{time_phrase}. I remember because I was already listening for something to go wrong.
You are asking about prior contact like it can be folded into one clean sentence. It cannot.
Contradiction? Memory frays when you love or fear the people inside it. Sometimes both.
Exact location? Near enough that I felt it in my body before I could name the geometry of it.
If you want me to blame the victim, you are asking the wrong witness. Intimate knowledge does not make cruelty easier to file.
Circumstance matters because intimate lives rot in small increments before they break in public.
People hurt the ones nearest to them while insisting they are protecting something precious. I have heard that language before.
Accidental may explain the spark. It does not explain the hand that stayed afterward.

choices.text: Ask whether someone tried to fix it after

nodes.text: Yes. Sudden enough to wound. Not sudden enough to keep guilt from making plans right after.
That part I believe without reservation. Somebody stayed to edit the truth before they left it.
